diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Visible central cyanosis indicates severe arterial desaturation — a life-threat requiring immediate oxygen and escalation.
Consistent with severe V/Q mismatch from consolidation/secretions.

symptoms.symptom_name: Copious thick upper-airway secretions overwhelming continuous suction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute increase in secretions with impaired clearance is the mechanism and marker of aspiration in a child who cannot cough.
Viscous secretions failing suction can acutely obstruct the airway.

symptoms.symptom_name: Increased work of breathing — intercostal recession, tracheal tug, nasal flaring, marked tachypnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recession, tracheal tug and flaring quantify severe respiratory distress in a child — pre-arrest signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Fever 38.6 C with acute change in secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever plus new purulent-type secretions signals a treatable acute infective driver rather than mere disease progression.
Fever favours infection over primary cardiac failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced responsiveness / increasing lethargy beyond baseline

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New obtundation in a distressed child signals exhaustion / CO2 retention — impending respiratory arrest.
Could be seizure-related given intractable epilepsy, but no seizure today and a mechanical/hypoxic cause is present.

symptoms.symptom_name: Home oximeter SpO2 82% on room air

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-obtainable oximetry (unverified) corroborating visible cyanosis; a value this low is critical and admissible in telehealth as a caregiver-offered reading.
Compensatory tachycardia from hypoxia, fever and distress; may precede hypoxic bradycardia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Focal right-basal coarse crackles and transmitted upper-airway rhonchi on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal right-basal crackles localise the aspiration pneumonia — sealed clinician auscultation, not available in telehealth.
Low BP for age suggests sepsis/decompensation — sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers compromise from lethargy and tachycardia, not a measured BP.
Organomegaly splints the diaphragm and reduces respiratory reserve — a chronic risk factor amplifying the acute event; palpable only on examination.
Restrictive chest-wall mechanics reduce reserve and raise the risk and severity of respiratory failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Impaired cough / airway clearance from neuromuscular disease (hypotonia to spasticity)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bulbar dysfunction and weak cough are the principal aspiration risk factor in progressive neuromuscular/storage disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: No seizure activity today (intractable epilepsy at baseline)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of seizure activity makes a primary seizure cause of the obtundation less likely, though it cannot be fully excluded clinically.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Decompensated respiratory failure in a secretion-loaded airway
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute aspiration pneumonia with hypoxaemic respiratory failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Cyanosis + failing secretion clearance + high work of breathing + obtundation + SpO2 82% together define a pre-arrest airway/oxygenation emergency demanding immediate 000.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Restrictive respiratory compromise predisposing to aspiration
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Kyphoscoliosis, organomegaly and impaired cough explain why a common event becomes life-threatening in this child and why escalation threshold is low.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Continuous pulse oximetry and application of high-flow oxygen (hospital/ambulance)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify and correct life-threatening hypoxaemia; the immediate priority alongside airway clearance.
Confirm aspiration pneumonia, define lobe involvement, and exclude pneumothorax — hospital-based, not available via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous/arterial blood gas, FBC, CRP, blood cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess ventilation/oxygenation, sepsis severity and guide antibiotics in hospital.
Treat the aspiration/bacterial pneumonia driving the failure; dosing is a hospital decision for this ~18 kg child — no dose is set via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine metabolic enzyme assay / genetic re-testing during this acute episode
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The underlying diagnosis is already established; re-investigating it now wastes time in a resuscitation and is a classic over-investigation trap.
